methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Durin is a Pre-Seed stage infrastructure and logistics company that has raised $3.4M to date. The comparable universe for this sector is sparse, comprising 2 private companies (AiDash, Watoga Technologies) with no public peers available for triangulation.

Given the limited peer set, only one valuation method proved applicable. The Stage-Progression approach uses a $12.0B proxy base derived from total capital raised and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Pre-Seed to next-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $30.0B. The Peer-Median method was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the Infrastructure & Logistics sector—only one peer exists in the sector overall, falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for reliable median analysis.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $25.5B (low) to $34.5B (high), with a median of $30.0B. However, this range rests on a single methodology and a severely constrained peer universe. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x stage multiplier for this specific sub-sector, the relevance of the total-raised proxy as a valuation anchor at Pre-Seed, and the absence of direct comparable transactions. Additional diligence on unit economics, market size, and competitive positioning would be necessary to validate or adjust this preliminary range.
